what_it_does: Defines CSS custom properties (design tokens) for the ConceptV system — colors, warm paper surfaces, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, motion, and layout primitives — plus reusable semantic type-preset classes and an opt-in global scope class.
observations: Paper-first warm palette centered on off-cream --paper (#FCFAF2) with gold and bronze accents.
Semantic surfaces are generated with color-mix over --paper using a single --zone-strength dial.
Legacy aliases such as --bg-canvas and --bg-surface are preserved with comments warning not to remove them.
Strict 8px spacing scale with named steps from --s-1 to --s-24.
Type ramp uses Sora for display, DM Sans for body, and JetBrains Mono for code.
Includes helper classes like .cv-h1, .cv-body, .cv-eyebrow, and an opt-in .cv-scope wrapper.
Shadows are intentionally quiet and warm rather than high-contrast.
No code symbols are declared in this file.
standouts: The --zone-strength dial that scales every semantic surface tone from a single value.
Use of color-mix to derive all semantic surfaces from the same paper base.
A warm, paper-first palette that reserves white for content cards and gold for active decisions.
Strict 8px spacing rhythm presented as the sole scale for the system.
Semantic typography classes with a clear .cv- prefix convention.
conventions: CSS custom properties declared in :root as the token layer.
Class names prefixed with .cv- for ConceptV typography utilities.
Section comments group tokens by category (surfaces, ink, accents, shadows, spacing, type, etc.).
Legacy aliases kept alongside new tokens for backward compatibility.
Opt-in global styling via .cv-scope rather than bare element selectors.
8px grid for spacing and a limited, named type scale.
concerns: Spacing scale contains duplicate values (--s-7/--s-8 both 32px, --s-9/--s-10 both 40px), which may be intentional aliases or accidental redundancy.
--surface-vi-edge references --accent-gold-dashed, whose name implies a dashed style but holds a solid color value.
.cv-scope sets a 16px base font size while --fs-base is 13px, creating a potential mismatch with the stated UI default.
Surfaces rely on color-mix, which may need fallback planning for older browsers.
The file mixes pure tokens, utility classes, and global defaults, blurring the boundary between a token library and a stylesheet.
notes: This is the central CSS token file for the ConceptV design system, encoding the warm paper palette, tonal surface zoning, type scale, and reusable typography classes.
questions: Which UI zones map to each --surface-* token in the actual application?
Is --zone-strength meant to be user-tunable, theme-tunable, or build-time only?
Are the duplicate spacing values intentional aliases or mistakes?
Where is .cv-scope applied — on <body>, on a canvas wrapper, or per surface?
How does the app.jsx accent recolor interact with the static tokens defined here?
Are fallbacks provided for browsers that do not support color-mix?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a source-of-truth token file imported once; the file fulfills that but also ships semantic type classes and an opt-in global scope, making it part token library and part light utility stylesheet.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for tokens
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a unified, tunable visual system where subtle surface differences are controlled by one dial, typography and spacing are standardized across surfaces, and legacy aliases prevent breakage while new tokens take over.
novelty: The --zone-strength dial combined with color-mix to generate a family of semantic surfaces from a shared paper base, plus the explicit 'felt, not shouted' zoning philosophy.
contribution: Provides a comprehensive, documented set of CSS custom properties and typography utility classes that encode the ConceptV visual language — warm paper surfaces, gold accent, strict spacing, type ramp — for use across deck, UI kit, and app surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS design-token file defining the ConceptV color palette, tonal surface zoning variables, typography scale, spacing, radii, shadows, motion, layout primitives, and opt-in typography utility classes.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth for visual design tokens and reusable type presets across ConceptV surfaces.
